I'm the leading contributor to Spack. I also happen to do geospatial research! I wrote the build recipes for and maintain most of the geospatial packages in Spack, including libraries like [GDAL](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/gdal/package.py), [GEOS](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/geos/package.py), [GMT](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/gmt/package.py), [GRASS](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/grass/package.py), [libgeotiff](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/libgeotiff/package.py), [PROJ](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/proj/package.py), and [QGIS](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/qgis/package.py), as well as dozens of Python libraries like [cartopy](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-cartopy/package.py), [fiona](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-fiona/package.py), [geopandas](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-geopandas/package.py), [geoplot](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-geoplot/package.py), [geopy](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-geopy/package.py), [pygeos](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-pygeos/package.py), [pyproj](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-pyproj/package.py), [rasterio](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-rasterio/package.py), and [shapely](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-shapely/package.py).

Unlike other packages managers, Spack allows users to completely control how a package is built. For example, if I want to build GDAL 3.2.3 with the Intel compilers, adding support for HDF4/5, and using a specific version of PROJ, I can get this like so:
```console
$ spack install gdal@3.2.3 %intel@19.1.2 +hdf4+hdf5 ^proj@7.1.0
```
Of course, if users don't care about the details, they can also just run `spack install gdal` and Spack will choose good defaults. Spack is designed for supercomputers, but can also run on your laptop. I personally use it to install all of my geospatial software in the cloud.

We at Spack would love to collaborate with you to make our builds even more robust. Would you or anyone else from your team be interested in helping maintain any of our build recipes for geospatial software? You don't have to know anything about Spack, it just gives us an expert to ping when a user reports build issues or submits a PR to modify the build recipe for that package. Of course, I'll still be helping to maintain these packages, so I can handle any Spack-specific questions.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/4025/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/4025/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/comments/1136105416","html_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/issues/4025#issuecomment-1136105416","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/4025","id":1136105416,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAF3Q3c5Dt5fI","user":{"login":"adamjstewart","id":12021217,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMDIxMjE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12021217?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart","html_url":"https://github.com/adamjstewart","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-05-24T15:54:21Z","updated_at":"2022-05-24T15:54:21Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Yes, I set our defaults to build the smallest possible subset of GDAL to improve build times. Since we build everything from source, a minimal compilation can take 5 min and involve 5 dependencies, but using the same defaults as Autotools/CMake could take hours and involve hundreds of dependencies. @tgamblin is planning to start aggressively offering pre-compiled binaries on as many platforms as we can, so once that happens I'm planning on changing the defaults to be more useful. the bug**

I'm experiencing connection/kernel issues with my local copy of Jupyter. I'm not sure if this is the right place to report this, but I encounter the issue with both `jupyter lab` and `jupyter notebook` so it may be an issue with a common core/server dependency.

**To Reproduce**

1. Launch Jupyter with `jupyter lab` or `jupyter notebook`
2. Create a new notebook with a simple `print('hello world')` line
3. Try to run this line
4. Nothing happens, errors in log

**Expected behavior**

I would Jupyter to find and use the kernel and run my code properly.

Having said that, I am much more in favor of asking people to select the build variant themselves To add some context, in [TorchGeo](https://github.com/microsoft/torchgeo), we have a single `environment.yml` in which we list the dependencies of the project. We would love to always specify `pytorch-gpu` as a dependency so that a GPU-capable version of PyTorch is installed even if the user doesn't have a GPU, but there is no version of `pytorch-gpu` available with Windows support, so we have to rely on `pytorch` instead. I think your point makes sense for command-line installs, but doesn't make sense for `environment.yml` installs. We _could_ have two `environment.yml` (the only difference being `pytorch-gpu` vs. `pytorch-cpu`, but this becomes yet another file in which we need to list the project's many dependencies and keep it up-to-date.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pytorch-cpu-feedstock/issues/comments/1138811319/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I think there is a way to specify more things about the architecture in your yaml file (but I am not sure) and I am not sure if conda supports the pip-esque sys.platform control flow https://peps.python.org/pep-0496/#examples From what I can tell this isn't possible in [`environment.yml`](https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-channels.html), only in a build recipe [`meta.yaml`](https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda-build/en/latest/resources/define-metadata.html). Also, the `meta.yaml` has a lot of limitations. You can't add a dependency specific to a single version of Python without converting the generic pure-Python universal build to a platform/Python-version-specific build. Overall, conda has very poor support for conditional dependencies compared to pip/spack.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pytorch-cpu-feedstock/issues/comments/1138865639/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/5809","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/5809/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/5809/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/5809/events","html_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/issues/5809","id":1251755402,"node_id":"I_kwDOAF3Q3c5KnEWK","number":5809,"title":"CMake: mismatch between docs and driver flags","user":{"login":"adamjstewart","id":12021217,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMDIxMjE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12021217?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart","html_url":"https://github.com/adamjstewart","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-29T00:45:21Z","updated_at":"2022-05-29T00:45:21Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I'm working on updating [Spack](https://spack.io)'s GDAL package for the new CMake build system (which is amazing btw). I noticed a few inconsistencies between the docs and the actual behavior I encountered w.r.t. driver flags.

## Required vs. optional drivers

According to https://gdal.org/build_hints.html#selection-of-drivers, `-DGDAL_BUILD_OPTIONAL_DRIVERS=OFF -DOGR_BUILD_OPTIONAL_DRIVERS=OFF` should "Globally disable all GDAL/raster or OGR/vector drivers". However, I still see several drivers being built:
```
-- Enabled drivers and features and found dependency packages
-- The following features have been enabled:

 * gdal_ISO8211, iso8211 library
 * gdal_GTIFF, GeoTIFF image format
 * gdal_MEM, Read/write data in Memory
 * gdal_VRT, Virtual GDAL Datasets
 * gdal_HFA, Erdas Imagine .img
 * ogr_MEM, Read/write driver for MEMORY virtual files
 * ogr_GEOJSON, GeoJSON/ESRIJSON/TopoJSON driver
 * ogr_TAB, MapInfo TAB and MIF/MID
 * ogr_SHAPE, ESRI shape-file
 * ogr_KML, KML
 * ogr_VRT, VRT - Virtual Format
```
I think this one just needs clarification in the docs that these particular drivers are not optional, they are always built.

## Driver names in docs do not match CMake flags

According to https://gdal.org/build_hints.html#selection-of-drivers, I can enable/disable all drivers by their driver name. However, some CMake flags do not match the names listed in https://gdal.org/drivers/vector/index.html:

* MITAB: CMake flags calls it TAB, not MITAB
* ESRI Shapefile: CMake flags calls it SHAPE, not ESRI_SHAPEFILE

## Undocumented drivers

CMake seems to be building the following driver:
```
* gdal_ISO8211, iso8211 library
```
But this driver is not documented at https://gdal.org/drivers/raster/index.html.

## Documented, but flag is not respected

In Spack we would like to be able to explicitly enable/disable all drivers. However, many of the drivers listed at https://gdal.org/drivers/raster/index.html and https://gdal.org/drivers/vector/index.html do not seem to have corresponding CMake flags:
```

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884315345","pull_request_review_id":988656379,"id":884315345,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40tZTR","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,68 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "RST" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_IDRISI.
+
+        - the "ElasticSearch" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_ELASTIC.
+
+        - the "PostgreSQL" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_PG.
+
+        - the "UK .NTF" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_NTF.
+
+    .. note::
+
+        Drivers that have both a raster and vector side (and are internally implemented by a
+        single GDALDriver instance) are controlled by either a GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name>
+        option or a OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name> one, but not both:
+
+        - The netCDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NETCDF option.
+        - The PDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDF option.
+        - The GPKG drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_GPKG option.
+        - The SQLite drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_SQLITE {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884316067","pull_request_review_id":988656942,"id":884316067,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40tZej","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,68 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "RST" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_IDRISI.
+
+        - the "ElasticSearch" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_ELASTIC.
+
+        - the "PostgreSQL" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_PG.
+
+        - the "UK .NTF" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_NTF.
+
+    .. note::
+
+        Drivers that have both a raster and vector side (and are internally implemented by a
+        single GDALDriver instance) are controlled by either a GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name>
+        option or a OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name> one, but not both:
+
+        - The netCDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NETCDF option.
+        - The PDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDF option.
+        - The GPKG drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_GPKG option.
+        - The SQLite drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_SQLITE {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884317290","pull_request_review_id":988657750,"id":884317290,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40tZxq","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,68 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "RST" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_IDRISI.
+
+        - the "ElasticSearch" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_ELASTIC.
+
+        - the "PostgreSQL" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_PG.
+
+        - the "UK .NTF" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_NTF.
+
+    .. note::
+
+        Drivers that have both a raster and vector side (and are internally implemented by a
+        single GDALDriver instance) are controlled by either a GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name>
+        option or a OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name> one, but not both:
+
+        - The netCDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NETCDF option.
+        - The PDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDF option.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884317401","pull_request_review_id":988657817,"id":884317401,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40tZzZ","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,68 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "RST" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_IDRISI.
+
+        - the "ElasticSearch" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_ELASTIC.
+
+        - the "PostgreSQL" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_PG.
+
+        - the "UK .NTF" vector driver is controlled by OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_NTF.
+
+    .. note::
+
+        Drivers that have both a raster and vector side (and are internally implemented by a
+        single GDALDriver instance) are controlled by either a GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name>
+        option or a OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_<driver_name> one, but not both:
+
+        - The netCDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NETCDF option.
+        - The PDF drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDF option.
+        - The GPKG drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_GPKG option.
+        - The SQLite drivers are controlled by the OGR_ENABLE_DRIVER_SQLITE {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884319535","pull_request_review_id":988659347,"id":884319535,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40taUv","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,86 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS2, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The AAIGRID, GRASSASCIIGRID and ISG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AAIGRID option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The vector EEDA and raster EEDAI drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_EEDA option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The HDF5 and BAG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_HDF5 option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The NWT_GRD and NWT_GRC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NORTHWOOD option.
+
+        - The SRP and ADRG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ADRG option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "AIG" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AIGRID.
+
+        - the "ENVISAT" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ESAT.
+
+        - the "GEOR" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GEORASTER.","path":"doc/source/build_hints.rst","position":59,"original_position":59,"commit_id":"ad90f5a1e253992dbc298dc245f9749a63a6bc31","original_commit_id":"ad90f5a1e253992dbc298dc245f9749a63a6bc31","user":{"login":"adamjstewart","id":12021217,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMDIxMjE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12021217?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart","html_url":"https://github.com/adamjstewart","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adamjstewart/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I believe these two are reversed:

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/pulls/comments/884320078","pull_request_review_id":988659725,"id":884320078,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Q3c40tadO","diff_hunk":"@@ -1803,11 +1803,86 @@ The following options are available to select a subset of drivers:
     Independently of options that control global behavior, drivers can be individually
     enabled or disabled with those options.
 
+    .. note::
+
+        <driver_name> above and below is *generally*, but not systematically the short driver name.
+
+        Some drivers may also be grouped together for build purposes.
+
+        - A number of "raw" raster drivers (ACE2, BT, BYN, CPG, CTable2, DIPX, DOQ1,
+          DOQ2, EHDR, EIR, ENVI, FAST, GenBIN, GSC, GTX, MFF2, ISCE, KRO, MFF, LAN,
+          LCP, LOSLAS, NDF, NTv2, PAUX, PNM, ROIPAC, RRASTER, SNODAS) are controlled
+          by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_RAW option.
+
+        - Planetary raster formats (PDS, PDS4, ISIS2, ISIS3, VICAR) are controlled by
+          the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_PDS option.
+
+        - The AAIGRID, GRASSASCIIGRID and ISG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AAIGRID option.
+
+        - The ECW and JP2ECW raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ECW option.
+
+        - The vector EEDA and raster EEDAI drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_EEDA option.
+
+        - The GSAG, GSBG and GS7BG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_GSG option.
+
+        - The HDF5 and BAG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_HDF5 option.
+
+        - The MrSID and JP2MrSID raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_MRSID option.
+
+        - The NITF, RPFTOC and ECRGTOC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NITF option.
+
+        - The NWT_GRD and NWT_GRC raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_NORTHWOOD option.
+
+        - The SRP and ADRG raster drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ADRG option.
+
+        - The Interlis 1 and Interlis 2 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ILI option.
+
+        - The WFS and OAPIF vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_WFS option.
+
+        - The AVCBIN and AVCE00 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AVC option.
+
+        - The DWG and DGNv8 vector drivers are controlled by the GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_DWG option.
+
+        There might be variations in naming, e.g. :
+
+        - the "AIG" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_AIGRID.
+
+        - the "ENVISAT" raster driver is controlled by GDAL_ENABLE_DRIVER_ESAT.
+
+        - the "GEOR" raster driver is controlled by I noticed a few inconsistencies between the docs and the actual behavior I encountered w.r.t. driver flags.

## Required vs. optional drivers

According to https://gdal.org/build_hints.html#selection-of-drivers, `-DGDAL_BUILD_OPTIONAL_DRIVERS=OFF -DOGR_BUILD_OPTIONAL_DRIVERS=OFF` should "Globally disable all GDAL/raster or OGR/vector drivers". However, I still see several drivers being built:
```
-- Enabled drivers and features and found dependency packages
-- The following features have been enabled:

 * gdal_ISO8211, iso8211 library
 * gdal_GTIFF, GeoTIFF image format
 * gdal_MEM, Read/write data in Memory
 * gdal_VRT, Virtual GDAL Datasets
 * gdal_HFA, Erdas Imagine .img
 * ogr_MEM, Read/write driver for MEMORY virtual files
 * ogr_GEOJSON, GeoJSON/ESRIJSON/TopoJSON driver
 * ogr_TAB, MapInfo TAB and MIF/MID
 * ogr_SHAPE, ESRI shape-file
 * ogr_KML, KML
 * ogr_VRT, VRT - Virtual Format
```
I think this one just needs clarification in the docs that these particular drivers are not optional, they are always built.

Interestingly, `gdalinfo` reports a different subset of drivers:
```console
$ gdalinfo --formats
Supported Formats:
  VRT -raster,multidimensional raster- (rw+v): Virtual Raster
  DERIVED -raster- (ro): Derived datasets using VRT pixel functions
  GTiff -raster- (rw+vs): GeoTIFF
  COG -raster- (wv): Cloud optimized GeoTIFF generator
  HFA -raster- (rw+v): Erdas Imagine Images (.img)
  MEM -raster,multidimensional raster- (rw+): In Memory Raster
```

## Driver names in docs do not match CMake flags

According to https://gdal.org/build_hints.html#selection-of-drivers, I can enable/disable all drivers by their driver name. However, some CMake flags do not match the names listed in https://gdal.org/drivers/vector/index.html:

* MITAB: CMake flags calls it TAB, not MITAB
* ESRI Shapefile: CMake flags calls it SHAPE, not ESRI_SHAPEFILE

## Undocumented drivers

CMake seems to be building the following driver:
```
* gdal_ISO8211, iso8211 library
```
But this driver is not documented at https://gdal.org/drivers/raster/index.html.

## Documented, but flag is not respected

In Spack we would like to be able to explicitly enable/disable all drivers. However, many of the drivers listed at https://gdal.org/drivers/raster/index.html and https://gdal.org/drivers/vector/index.html do not seem to have corresponding CMake flags:
```
